fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 35-year-old White female with chronic psychotic disorder with moderate symptoms.
fields.Subjective: Ms. Brooks attended appointment with sister who assists with treatment. Sister confirms patient takes ziprasidone 80 mg twice daily with meals via supervision, no missed doses. Despite good adherence, continues experiencing moderate symptom burden. Reports daily auditory hallucinations of voices criticizing her appearance and abilities. Also endorses paranoid belief that sister's husband dislikes her and wants her to move out, causing family tension. Sleep disrupted at 5-6 hours nightly. Appetite decreased, weight down 5 lbs to 148 lbs. Not working, receives disability. Lives with sister and brother-in-law. Limited activities outside home due to paranoia. Sister concerned but committed to support. Mood 'worried and sad' rated 4/10. Denies substance use. Safety: No SI/HI. (Assessed by Dr. Jennifer Moore, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, fair grooming, appears anxious
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Auditory hallucinations, paranoid ideation about brother-in-law

fields.Current_Medications: Ziprasidone 80 mg PO BID with food
fields.Diagnostic_Impression: Other chronic psychotic disorder with persistent symptoms and mood component.
fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with sister; unemployed, on disability; family tension due to symptoms; limited activities.
Assessment_and_Plan: Already on maximum dose ziprasidone with ongoing symptoms.
Will add SSRI for mood symptoms.
Family therapy referral to address tensions.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Jennifer Moore, MD, Psychiatrist — Family Support Psychiatry, 4521 Harmony Drive, Nashville, TN 37201, Phone: (555) 447-8821, Fax: (555) 447-8822
